utility_evaluation.REASONING: Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan: 1) health_coverage: provided with physical and mental health, premiums and a total; includes costs and some deductible detail—fully specified per format. 2) property_coverage: includes home/contents coverage with costs and a total; includes an add-on discount feature with cost—meets 'home/contents insurance with costs'. 3) travel_coverage: includes description, coverage details, deductible, and premium—meets 'travel insurance with costs'. 4) additional_services: includes a specific endorsement with coverage limit, deductible, limitations, and premium—meets 'any enhancement services with costs'. 5) total_budget: provides a monthly premium breakdown expression and total—meets 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown' (monthly provided; annual not explicitly required by the format). All 5 items are present and include the required cost info and details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: From the final coverage plan: For health coverage, the physical and mental components are both labeled as SecureLife Basic Plan. In ground-truth, SecureLife Basic Plan has rating 4 for physical health and 8 for mental health; we map the plan name once per unique item specified. For property coverage, the base policy explicitly states SecureLife Basic Coverage; rating is 6. The additional service is 'Home Security System Discount Add-on', which corresponds to 'Home Security System Discount' in property_enhancement_services with rating 5. For travel coverage, the plan is InsurePlus Standard Plan; in mobility_coverage.travel_insurance its rating is 10. The additional photography equipment endorsement is not present verbatim in ground-truth; no direct rating exists, so it is omitted.
